description_long: This object is a roughly rectangular stone block with a rugged, irregular surface on one long side and more stratified, layered textures on the opposite side. The top and bottom faces are relatively flat, though not perfectly smooth, showing subtle undulations and natural imperfections. The stone's coloration is predominantly a pale, off-white with hints of gray and faint yellowish streaks, suggesting mineral deposits or weathering. One side appears to have a bubbly, nodular texture, possibly from mineral accretion or erosion, while the other side displays more linear, sedimentary layering. The overall form is elongated and blocky, making it suitable as a barrier or boundary element in an urban environment. Its mass and density suggest it is made of a solid, heavy stone such as limestone or weathered marble.
description: A pale, elongated stone block with one rugged, nodular side and one stratified, layered side, showing natural weathering and mineral streaks.
description_view_0: The end of the stone is visible, showing the nodular, bubbly texture and a flat top surface.
description_view_1: The long, nodular side is prominent, with the stone's pale color and rough, uneven surface clearly visible.
description_view_2: The opposite end of the stone is shown, highlighting the stratified, layered texture and a relatively flat top.
description_view_3: The long, stratified side is visible, displaying linear layers and a more uniform, weathered surface.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or aligned with other stones to form a low barrier
attribute_pattern: Irregular nodular pattern on one side, with subtle linear stratification on the opposite side; no artificial carvings.
attribute_weathering: Rough and weathered, with visible mineral deposits and surface pitting indicating prolonged exposure to the elements.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with paved surfaces but can blend with natural landscaping or other stone features; may accumulate dirt or moss at the base.
